# Landing Page Modernization Tech Debt

## Story Ownership Decision

- Best story for adding a minimal `id="contact"` anchor section so the hero primary CTA always lands on-page:
	- [Contact CTA and Footer — Modern Landing Page Redesign](./user-stories/contact-cta-footer-landing-page.md)

Rationale:
- This story already owns the Contact section and footer anchor navigation contract.
- It keeps section-target ownership in one place instead of spreading contact behavior across unrelated stories.

## Outstanding Test Coverage Debt

- Add Playwright coverage for the listed hero and reduced-motion cases to lock in the redesign behavior.

Source requirements:
- [Hero Section Technical Spec](./specs/hero-section-spec.md)
- [Accessibility, Animation, and Cross-Device Quality Story](./user-stories/accessibility-animation-quality-landing-page.md)

Minimum Playwright scope:
- Hero renders on `/` with visible headline (`.hero-headline`).
- Two hero CTAs exist and target `#contact` and `#services`.
- Three value chips (`.value-chip`) are present.
- Primary CTA navigation scrolls to the contact target once contact section is implemented.
- Secondary CTA navigation scrolls to services target.
- Mobile viewport (375px width) has no horizontal overflow and stacked hero CTAs.
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables reveal transitions and shows `.reveal-item` content immediately.

Done criteria:
- Tests are committed and executed in CI.
- Failing tests clearly identify hero/reduced-motion regressions.
